gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/128)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/128)
text: ذُكِرَ أَصْحَابُنَا عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقُلْتُ مَا أَتَغَدَّى وَ لَا أَتَعَشَّى إِلَّا وَ مَعِيَ مِنْهُمُ الِاثْنَانِ وَ الثَّلَاثَةُ وَ أَقَلُّ وَ أَكْثَرُ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَضْلُهُمْ عَلَيْكَ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ

فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ كَيْفَ وَ أَنَا أُطْعِمُهُمْ طَعَامِي وَ أُنْفِقُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ مَالِي وَ أُخْدِمُهُمْ عِيَالِي فَقَالَ إِنَّهُمْ إِذَا دَخَلُوا عَلَيْكَ دَخَلُوا بِرِزْقٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ كَثِيرٍ وَ إِذَا خَرَجُوا خَرَجُوا بِالْمَغْفِرَةِ لَكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abu Muhammad Al Wabishy who said,

‘Our companions were mentioned in the presence of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, so I said, ‘I neither have lunch nor dinner except there are two of them with me, and the three, and less and more’. So Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Their merit upon you is greater than your merit upon them’.

So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! How come and I am feeding them my food, and spending upon them from my wealth, and my dependants are serving them?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘They, when they come over to you, enter with a lot of sustenance from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, and when they exit, they go out with the Forgiveness for you’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu Muhammad al-Wabishi who has said the following: “Our people were mentioned before abu ‘Abd Allah, recipient of divine supreme covenant. I said, ‘I do not eat lunch or dinner without two, three or more or less of them with me.’ Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) said, ‘Their merit over you is greater than your merit over them.’ I then said, ‘May Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, how can it be as such? I feed them and spent for them from my property and my family serves them.’ The Imam said, ‘When they come to you they come with plenty of sustenance from Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, and when they leave, they do so with forgiveness for you.’”
